behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (request builder -> API client), not pure algorithm only. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no real network/OS needed; a stub transport that records outbound payload catches missing labels. LLM-DEP: rejected because behavior targets deterministic plumbing: "request sent ... includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels"; this excludes dependence on model output content (only payload fields are asserted). MULTI-STEP: no.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier module to a fake API client that captures outgoing request JSON. Invoke classify() with a taxonomy, then assert outbound payload contains every taxonomy label exactly once and in expected field.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: boundary is response parsing/validation across modules (API adapter -> classifier result). REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: stubbed API response is sufficient; real network adds little for this failure. LLM-DEP rejected with deterministic-plumbing quote: "returns \"unknown\" instead of forwarding the unexpected value"; exclusion quote: behavior is membership validation against fixed taxonomy, not correctness of model semantics. MULTI-STEP: no.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fake API adapter returning an out-of-taxonomy label (e.g., "medical"). Call classify() and assert returned value is exactly "unknown" and raw provider label is not propagated.

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is one local normalization function. REAL-INFRA: none. LLM-DEP rejected by text: "identical raw API responses" and "normalization step always produces the same final label"; this is deterministic transformation of fixed input, not model judgment quality. MULTI-STEP: no.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization function repeatedly with the same raw response fixture (including casing/whitespace variants encoded in fixture) and assert identical normalized output each run.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: explicit dependence on repeated live model outputs; nondeterminism is the failure surface. MOCK-HIDE: stubbed responses would always look consistent and miss real drift/variance. REAL-INFRA exists (real API), but Agentic is the tighter boundary because correctness depends on model decision content. MULTI-STEP: single-step repeated calls, so not Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Run N real classification calls with identical input against the live provider, logging raw provider outputs and final labels. Assert final label is stable across runs when raw provider output is unchanged; flag instability attributable to model/output variance.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: budget policy is enforced at handoff between tokenizer/policy/request-dispatch modules. REAL-INFRA: can be tested in-process with fake dispatcher; no real network/OS required. LLM-DEP rejected with deterministic-plumbing quote: "enforces the configured token budget before dispatch"; exclusion quote: this checks pre-dispatch policy application, not model output content. MULTI-STEP: no.
behaviors.test_description: Configure a small token budget and inject an over-budget document. Verify module applies configured policy (truncate or reject) and that fake dispatcher sees only compliant requests (or no dispatch on reject).

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: Execution requires real AI API call and provider usage accounting; mock usage values would trivially pass and hide real overage. By default this is Agentic because behavior depends on real model call outcomes/usage metadata, not just routing plumbing. System considered but rejected: behavior is about model-call cost behavior, not merely HTTP mechanics.
behaviors.test_description: Send representative documents to the live provider with production request template, collect provider-reported prompt/completion/total tokens, and assert total tokens <= configured budget across repeated trials.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: retry behavior sits at module boundary (API client + retry/backoff policy + caller contract). REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: fake API returning rate-limit errors plus controllable clock is enough; real network timing is not required to catch logic bugs. LLM-DEP rejected with deterministic-plumbing quote: "waits and retries rather than returning an error"; exclusion quote: assertion concerns transport/control-flow on rate-limit signal, not model output content. MULTI-STEP agent pipeline: no.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fake API adapter that returns rate-limit error on first K calls then success. Verify classifier waits according to backoff policy, retries, and eventually returns success without surfacing intermediate rate-limit error.
